concept.conceptName: Gamma glutamyl transferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

en.name: Gamma glutamyl transferase (GGT) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Gamma glutamyl transferase (GGT, also written GGTP or Gamma-GT) is a liver enzyme involved in the transfer of amino acids across the cellular membrane and in glutathione metabolism [1]. Its activity in blood rises in cholestatic and biliary conditions, and is also a sensitive (though not specific) marker of alcohol-related liver injury. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser on serum or plasma. It is collected in routine biochemistry as part of a liver panel, especially when investigating cholestasis, monitoring chronic liver disease, or following alcohol use. Results are reported as catalytic activity concentration; NPU recognises both **U/L** (the conventional unit, used by virtually all clinical labs) and **µkat/L** (the SI unit) [2]. The IFCC reference measurement procedure for gamma-GT at 37 °C, listed by JCTLM, gives the conversion factor `1 U/L = 0.01667 µkat/L` [3]. ## Included Concepts The set contains a single LOINC "Lab Test" concept, measured in serum or plasma — by far the only specimen used in routine practice for this assay. - *3026910 / 2324-2 — Gamma glutamyl transferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in U/L. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC: **`[U]/L`** (concept 8645). The notations `U/L` and `IU/L` are used interchangeably in clinical practice and represent the same value mathematically — no conversion is needed between them. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name, but the following LOINC concepts that exist for GGT are deliberately not part of this set: - **DBS (Dried Blood Spot)** — `36032012 / 96593-9`. GGT measured on dried blood spot is not interchangeable with serum/plasma values (whole-blood matrix dilutes plasma enzymes by the haematocrit fraction, ≈ 40-45%). It is rarely used in clinical practice and could be added to a separate concept set in the future if needed. - **Other specimens**: amniotic fluid, body fluid, dialysis fluid, semen, urine, 24-hour urine — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Macromolecular GGT presence** (`100741-8`) — a qualitative test for macro-GGT complexes, not a quantitative activity measurement. - **Ratios** like `Gamma glutamyl transferase/Aspartate aminotransferase` (`2325-9`) — out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "GGT", "gamma-GT", "γ-GT", "GGTP" or similar without further specification: `3026910 / 2324-2 — Gamma glutamyl transferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. This is by far the most widely used LOINC code for this test. - **Do not confuse with**: GGT/AST ratio, macromolecular GGT presence, or GGT in non-blood specimens (urine, body fluid, etc.) — all belong to other concept sets or are out of scope. - **Method is not part of the mapping target**: when the source records the assay method (kinetic colorimetric, IFCC reference at 37 °C, etc.), preserve it in the measurement source value — it does not change the target concept. - **Unit naming**: source systems may report `U/L` or `IU/L` (sometimes `iU/L`). All correspond to the same physical quantity. Map them all to the recommended unit `[U]/L` (concept 8645). Values reported in `µkat/L` should be converted using `1 U/L = 0.01667 µkat/L` (i.e. multiply µkat/L by ~60 to get U/L) [3]. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2324-2 — Gamma glutamyl transferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (Part description for "Gamma glutamyl transferase"). Available: https://loinc.org/2324-2/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/ [3] Joint Committee for Traceability in Laboratory Medicine (JCTLM). Database of higher-order reference measurement procedures, reference materials and reference measurement services. BIPM/IFCC/ILAC. Available: https://www.jctlmdb.org/#/app/home
